findings.summary: rolling=true silently overrides an explicit end_date in _effective_holdout_end (date.max); widening-only direction and pinned to null for this window by the declaration test, but a future declaration carrying both would treat the end as open. Consider forbidding rolling=true with non-null end_date.

generate_sealed_holdout_window_id now skips absent component fields; legacy IDs unaffected (end_date was previously required) and null-vs-absent canonicalize differently, but the ID component set is no longer fixed-shape.
GATE_INVENTORY.md still cites the superseded window_id holdwin_d5cba50af19976275ab26f34 (live inventory doc drift); RIGOR-P03 handoff is historical and correctly untouched.

Research venv resolves alpha_system to the MAIN repo src, not the worktree; pytest is safe via pyproject pythonpath=['src'] (phase validation genuine), but ad-hoc worktree scripts must set PYTHONPATH=<worktree>/src.

Contract test classifies locked-test locks via string compare start_ts >= '2025-01-01T00:00:00'; verified 0 misclassified across all 136 locks today, but non-zero-padded timestamps would misclassify silently.
